metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students develop programming logic and coding skills using languages such as Python or Java, learning algorithms, software development practices, and problem-solving approaches. This course emphasizes practical application and development of computational thinking skills.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in programming logic, algorithms, software development, and computational problem-solving.

big_ideas.statement: Programming enables computers to solve problems by implementing logic, algorithms, and procedures through coded instructions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding programming fundamentals—variables, control flow, functions—enables development of increasingly complex solutions.

big_ideas.statement: Computational thinking—breaking problems into components, identifying patterns, and designing algorithms—is applicable beyond programming.
big_ideas.elaboration: Developing algorithmic thinking builds problem-solving capacity that transfers to multiple contexts and disciplines.

big_ideas.statement: Software development requires planning, testing, debugging, and documentation to create reliable, maintainable programs.
big_ideas.elaboration: Professional development practices ensure code quality, enable collaboration, and support long-term program maintenance.

big_ideas.statement: Programming languages, frameworks, and tools continue to evolve, requiring continuous learning and adaptation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding programming principles transfers across languages and enables learning new tools and technologies throughout a career.

competencies.raw: Design algorithms and logical solutions for computational problems
i_can_statements: I can analyze problems and design algorithmic solutions
I can create flowcharts and pseudocode for algorithms
I can design data structures appropriate for problems
I can evaluate algorithms for efficiency and effectiveness

competencies.raw: Plan software projects with appropriate architecture and components
i_can_statements: I can design object-oriented program structures
I can plan program modules and functions
I can design user interfaces for programs
I can plan testing and debugging strategies

competencies.raw: Design programs that solve real-world problems
i_can_statements: I can identify problems that can be solved with programming
I can design programs addressing specific needs
I can plan programs incorporating user requirements
I can design scalable and maintainable solutions

competencies.raw: Evaluate program functionality and recommend improvements
i_can_statements: I can test programs against specifications
I can identify bugs and logic errors
I can analyze program performance
I can recommend code improvements and refactoring

competencies.raw: Write code using programming language syntax and conventions
i_can_statements: I can write code using appropriate language syntax
I can use variables, data types, and operators correctly
I can follow coding conventions and style standards
I can write readable and well-commented code

competencies.raw: Implement control flow and logical structures in programs
i_can_statements: I can use conditional statements (if, else, switch)
I can use loops for iteration and repetition
I can implement functions and modular code
I can use data structures: lists, arrays, dictionaries
I can identify syntax and logic errors
I can use debugging tools and techniques
I can test programs systematically
I can trace program execution to understand behaviour

competencies.raw: Document and communicate about programs and code
i_can_statements: I can write clear documentation for programs
I can create user guides and instructions
I can document code with appropriate comments
I can present and explain program functionality

competencies.raw: Use integrated development environments and programming tools
i_can_statements: I can use IDE features for coding and debugging
I can use version control systems for code management
I can use build and compilation tools
I can use libraries and frameworks appropriately

competencies.raw: Use programming languages effectively for various applications
i_can_statements: I can program in Python and/or Java
I can apply language features and idioms
I can use language-specific libraries and modules
I can transition between programming languages

competencies.raw: Apply technology for program testing and validation
i_can_statements: I can use testing frameworks and tools
I can write test cases and conduct testing
I can automate testing processes
I can use performance analysis tools

competencies.raw: Evaluate and apply emerging programming technologies
i_can_statements: I can assess new languages and frameworks
I can understand artificial intelligence and machine learning basics
I can evaluate technology tools for programming
I can learn and adapt to new programming technologies

areas.name: Programming Fundamentals
topics: Programming languages: Python, Java, and language selection

areas.name: Algorithms and Data Structures
topics: Stacks, queues, and other data structures
